data.content_ref: inline:{"claim": "The collapse resulted from an ontological revolution in Bronze Age political theology: the invention of transcendent monotheism (Atenism precursors, Yahwism) delegitimized immanent divine kingship and dissolved the ritual foundation of palace redistribution","mechanism": "Ideological innovation in Levantine and Anatolian merchant communities produced a 'disenchantment' of palace cosmology; rejection of divine kingship as ontologically necessary collapsed the performative legitimacy required for corvée labor and tax compliance; Egypt survived because Amarna heresy was ritually contained and traditional theology restored, preserving performative structure even under Ramesside contraction","scope": {"covers": ["Mycenaean Greece", "Hittite Anatolia", "Ugarit", "Levantine city-states", "palace ideological systems"], "excludes": ["materialist or environmental causation", "Sea Peoples military explanation", "systems-theoretic network collapse"]}, "forbidden": [{"case": "Contemporaneous textual evidence of mass religious conversion or theological polemic against divine kingship in palatial archives before collapse", "eval": "rubric:std-hist"}, {"case": "Archaeological evidence of iconoclasm directed at royal cult images in palace destruction layers predating military destruction", "eval": "rubric:std-hist"}, {"case": "Survival of immanent divine kingship in non-palatial polities (e.g., early Israelite chiefdoms) that should have been equally delegitimized", "eval": "rubric:std-hist"}, {"case": "Egyptian theological restoration under Ramesses II occurring after rather than before the peak collapse period, making containment chronologically implausible", "eval": "rubric:std-hist"}], "prose_notes": "Counterexample-first application testing whether ideological superstructure can override material base; exploits Weberian disenchantment model anachronistically; highly vulnerable to absence of pre-collapse theological dissent and to functionalist objections about material constraints on ideological change"}
